title: Deeming certain conduct of members of Antifa as domestic terrorism and designating Antifa as a domestic terrorist organization.
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
plain_summary: This bill wants to officially call Antifa a domestic terrorist group. The lawmakers say Antifa members have committed violent crimes and damaged property across the country. The bill asks the Department of Justice to arrest and prosecute Antifa members for terrorism crimes. It also wants the government to use all its tools and money to stop Antifa's activities.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution is about considering certain actions by members of Antifa as domestic terrorism and naming Antifa as a domestic terrorist organization.

sections.title: Whereas Clauses
sections.text: The House of Representatives lists many reasons for this resolution. They say Antifa is an extreme group motivated by communism, anarchism, socialism, and violence that has shown a commitment to breaking laws and criminal behavior. In two thousand sixteen, the Department of Homeland Security officially called Antifa activities domestic terrorist violence. One Antifa chapter called Rose City Antifa has said they will prevent their enemies from organizing by any means necessary and that fighting fascism sometimes requires physical fighting. The group has held signs saying they are ungovernable and want to abolish immigration enforcement, police, prisons, borders, and presidents. An Antifa member said in two thousand seventeen that sometimes you have to use direct action to stop enemies because protesting and yelling will not work, and you have to make them afraid. FBI Director Christopher Wray told Congress that people who identify with Antifa gather in small regional groups and are organized at that level, proving their violent attacks disguised as protests are coordinated events. The Congressional Research Service reports that Antifa literature tells followers to publish personal information like home addresses, phone numbers, photos, and social media profiles of their enemies. In June two thousand eighteen, at least one thousand five hundred Immigration and Customs Enforcement employees had their personal information published by an Antifa-linked social media account and received violent threats and harassment. In February two thousand seventeen, Antifa riots caused one hundred thousand dollars in damage at the University of California at Berkeley. That same month, over two hundred rioters were charged with attacking police officers, setting fire to a limousine, and breaking business windows, and other Antifa rioters had hammers, crowbars, bricks, rocks, flares, firecrackers, and other explosive devices. In August two thousand seventeen, about one hundred Antifa rioters caused violence during a peaceful rally in Berkeley, California, where they repeatedly punched and kicked innocent people, sending several peaceful protesters to the hospital, and thirteen rioters were arrested for assault with deadly weapons and blocking police officers. In July two thousand twenty, a self-proclaimed Antifa member named Willem Van Spronsen threw lit objects at vehicles and buildings, caused a car fire, tried to light a five hundred gallon propane tank, and tried to firebomb an Immigration and Customs Enforcement detention facility in Tacoma, Washington. In February two thousand twenty-one, the United States Attorney for Oregon said Antifa caused more than two million three hundred thousand dollars in riot damage to federal property in Oregon, not including damage to private businesses or state, county, and city buildings. During the summer of two thousand twenty, Antifa helped cause over two billion dollars in damage to churches, federal buildings, businesses, and other downtown buildings across twenty states, resulting in the death of at least thirty people and seven hundred injured police officers. During the summer and fall of two thousand twenty, Portland District Attorney Mike Schmidt refused to file charges in about seventy percent of the roughly one thousand protest and riot related cases brought by police officers. In May two thousand twenty, the Department of Justice officially called Antifa violence domestic terrorism in a public statement. A rioter named Malik Fard Muhammad was sentenced to ten years in federal prison for repeatedly endangering police officers' lives, destroying public property, and encouraging others to commit violence during Portland protests in two thousand twenty. In January two thousand twenty-one, Portland Police reported that Antifa rioters attacked officers in downtown Portland with commercial grade aerial fireworks and firebombs, and destroyed government property and over a dozen downtown businesses. In that same riot, police were also attacked with bricks, rocks, and frozen water bottles, and Portland Mayor Ted Wheeler reported at least tens of thousands of dollars in damage. In August two thousand twenty-two, Antifa defended the sexualization of children by guarding a kid-friendly drag show at a North Texas distillery. In January two thousand twenty-three, Atlanta police arrested six extremists on domestic terrorism charges after violent riots by Antifa members who broke windows, destroyed businesses, set fire to a police car, and vandalized city walls. That same month, Antifa told its followers to bring pipes, spray paint, kerosene, and lighters to New York City to burn it all down. In March two thousand twenty-three, Antifa rioters set fire to a future Atlanta police training facility by launching fireworks, Molotov cocktails, and other destructive items at police officers and into the construction site, resulting in twenty-three extremists being arrested on domestic terrorism charges. These coordinated destructive acts of hate by Antifa are attacks against the nation's law enforcement and the American people and have no place in the United States. Members of Antifa are relentlessly dedicated to using acts of domestic terrorism to suppress opposing political ideas.

sections.title: Resolution Provisions
sections.text: Therefore, the House of Representatives resolves that the conduct of Antifa members, or any unlawful conduct at an Antifa-affiliated demonstration, is considered to be domestic terrorism as defined in section two thousand three hundred thirty-one of title eighteen of the United States Code. The House designates Antifa, and any other affiliated group or subsidiary of Antifa, to be a domestic terrorist organization. The House calls on the Department of Justice to prosecute these crimes of domestic terrorism by Antifa as defined in section two thousand three hundred thirty-one of title eighteen of the United States Code, and to use all available tools and resources to combat the spread of domestic terrorism committed by Antifa.
